title: Poland Allocates â‚¬633m to Six Green Hydrogen Production Projects
content: Polandâ€™s Bank Gospodarstwa Krajowego (BGK) has allocated PLN 2.7 billion (EUR 633m/USD 733m) in non-repayable subsidies to six green hydrogen projects of at least 20 MW, funded under the National Recovery and Resilience Plan (KPO) to support RED III targets. The largest share, PLN 1.215 billion, goes to Orlenâ€™s Hydrogen Eagle project, while its affiliate Lotos Green H2 will receive PLN 523 million. Polenergiaâ€™s H2Silesia secures PLN 618 million, Tauron gets PLN 133 million for a facility in southern Silesia, Bioagra receives PLN 128 million, and Promet-Plast has been awarded PLN 123 million for an installation in Gaj Olawski near Wroclaw, where it launched Polandâ€™s first commercial green hydrogen electrolyser.
author: Analysts Account
